data.content:
The problem is classic: the target generates the file but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**. Globs expand at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

The fix: add the generated file to `Compile` and `FileWrites` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs before `CoreCompile` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, and also register it in `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    </ItemGroup>

data.content:
Build succeeds. Here's the diagnosis:

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit `**/*.cs` glob expands at **evaluation time** (before any targets execute), so files created during the build are invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly include the generated file in `Compile` (so it's compiled) and `FileWrites` (so `dotnet clean` removes it).
